about.headline: Advanced Research Training in Social Determinants of Health
about.lead: A mentored fellowship at Emory's Nell Hodgson Woodruff School of Nursing preparing the next generation of nurse scientists to confront the social drivers of health.
about.body:
The SDOH ART Fellowship is a national training program hosted by Emory University's Nell Hodgson Woodruff School of Nursing. Funded by the National Institute of Nursing Research (NIH/NINR), it equips nurse scientists and interdisciplinary researchers with the methods, mentorship, and frameworks to understand — and act on — how social, environmental, cultural, and policy conditions shape health across diverse populations.

## Who it's for

The fellowship supports scholars at two stages. **Early-career fellows** receive mentored training to grow into independent investigators with advanced SDOH research skills. **Mid-career fellows** integrate an SDOH lens into an established program of research, positioning them as leaders in the field.

## The cohorts

Funded as a three-year initiative, the fellowship has welcomed two cohorts to date — 2025 and 2026 — with a third to follow in 2027. Every fellow is matched with a dedicated Emory faculty mentor aligned to their research focus, and led by core faculty member Dr. Jill Hamilton.

benefits.title: Dedicated faculty mentorship
benefits.text: An NIH-funded Emory mentor matched to your research focus, guiding you throughout the program year.

benefits.title: Intensive five-day short course
benefits.text: An in-person short course in SDOH research methods at Emory's Schools of Nursing and Public Health.

benefits.title: Methods and data science
benefits.text: Hands-on training in quantitative, qualitative, and mixed methods, with access to Emory's Center for Data Science.

benefits.title: $750 travel award
benefits.text: Funding to present and disseminate your SDOH research at a national conference.

benefits.title: National peer network
benefits.text: Join a national, interdisciplinary cohort of nurse scientists advancing health equity.

benefits.title: Certificate and pathway
benefits.text: Earn a certificate of completion and a clear pathway toward independent, SDOH-focused research.

course.intro: At the center of the fellowship is an intensive five-day, in-person short course held at Emory each June, paired with year-long mentorship.

days.title: Frameworks and intersectionality
days.focus: SDOH overview, conceptual models, and building your own research framework; developing your problem statement.

days.title: Community-engaged research
days.focus: Ethics, cultural respect, recruitment, and trust-building through interactive case studies and role-play.

days.title: Research design and methods
days.focus: Quantitative, qualitative, and mixed-methods designs for SDOH, with a focus on rigor and matching questions to methods.

days.title: Pitching and innovation
days.focus: A "Shark Tank" of two-minute research pitches and a panel on AI and the social determinants of health.

days.title: Surviving academia and dissemination
days.focus: Funding pathways (F-to-K), building research teams, dissemination planning, and one-on-one mentoring.

eligibility: Early- and mid-career nurse scientists and interdisciplinary researchers.
Doctoral (PhD) students, postdoctoral fellows, and early- to mid-career faculty.
Research focused on — or pivoting toward — the social determinants of health.
Reference letters are required; applications without them are considered incomplete.
Final eligibility details are confirmed with each cohort announcement.

contact.note: For questions about eligibility, mentorship, or upcoming application cycles, reach out to the program team or send an inquiry.

framework.headline: The NHWSN Social Determinants of Health Framework
framework.intro: The Nell Hodgson Woodruff School of Nursing organizes the social determinants of health into four interconnected domains. Like pieces of a single puzzle, the domains overlap and reinforce one another to shape health outcomes. The framework gives fellows a shared language for naming the non-biological forces behind the disparities they study — and where intervention is possible.

domains.name: Social
domains.blurb: Daily living conditions and access to care.

domains.name: Environmental
domains.blurb: The places and conditions people live and work in.

domains.name: Policy
domains.blurb: The systems and structures that distribute resources.

framework.closing: Throughout the fellowship, fellows map their own research questions onto these domains — pinpointing the social drivers at play, designing studies that measure them, and proposing interventions that move outcomes. The framework anchors the program's case studies, gallery walks, and mentored project work.

inquiry.headline: Inquiries
inquiry.intro: Questions about the SDOH ART Fellowship, eligibility, or mentorship? Send us a note and the program team will respond.
